Editors and hiring teams

Freelance video editor rates

Rates make more sense when tied to deliverables, source footage, timeline, revision load, and the level of creative ownership required.

Scope drives rate

A simple social cut, a long-form YouTube episode, a brand launch edit, and a paid social variant package are different jobs. Budget should follow the actual deliverables.

Clarify ownership

Rates rise when the editor owns story structure, sound design, motion graphics, captions, color, music selection, versioning, and client review management.

Make revision load explicit

Project rates should include a defined number of revision rounds. Hourly work should include an expected weekly range and clear approval process.

Need an editor?

Send a hiring brief and start the early matching queue.
